messages.content: add a prepositional phrase to the end:His personal life contrasts with his private

life at work. 

His personal life contrasts with his private life at work behind closed doors.
grammatical structure:privacy and its contrast with the public and personal lives of politicians

Subject: privacy
Verb: contrasts
Object: with the public and personal lives of politicians

Overall, the sentence follows a subject-verb-object grammatical structure.
remove the subject:He live in the public eye
"Live in the public eye."
grammatical pattern for:live in the public eye

Subject: (implied - third person singular)
Verb: live
Object: in the public eye

The grammatical pattern is subject-verb-object-prepositional phrase.
grammatical structure for:live in the public eye

Subject: He (implied)
Verb: live
Object: in the public eye

The grammatical structure follows a subject-verb-object pattern.
grammatical structure for: deserve some privacy

Subject: (implied - You/We/They/He/She)
Verb: deserve
Object: some privacy

The grammatical structure follows a subject-verb-object pattern.
grammatical structure of: conduct a survey

Subject: (implied - You/We/They/He/She)
Verb: conduct
Object: a survey

The grammatical structure follows a subject-verb-object pattern.
In conduct a survey a mistake made by saying/dukt/ /sur/ would look like what
Saying /dukt/ /sur/ would be a mistake in terms of correct pronunciation. The correct pronunciation is /kənˈdʌkt/ /ˈsɜːrveɪ/.
infinitive of contrasts
The infinitive of "contrasts" is "to contrast".
definition of contrast as a verb
As a verb, the term "contrast" means to compare two or more things in order to highlight their differences. It can also mean to show a striking difference between two or more things.
If you show the contrast between two things you....: is contrast used as a verb
Yes, the word "contrast" is being used as a verb in the sentence "If you show the contrast between two things you...".
how much would five percent interest per year be on two billion dollars

To calculate the interest on $2 billion with a 5% annual interest rate, we can use the formula: 

Interest = Principal x Rate

where Principal is the amount of money ($2 billion), and Rate is the annual interest rate (5% or 0.05)

So, the interest for a year would be:

Interest = $2,000,000,000 x 0.05 = $100,000,000

Therefore, if the interest rate is 5% per year, the interest on $2 billion would be $100,000,000 for one year.
what would three percent be

To calculate the interest on $2 billion with a 3% annual interest rate, we can use the same formula as before: 

Interest = Principal x Rate

where Principal is the amount of money ($2 billion), and Rate is the annual interest rate (3% or 0.03)

So, the interest for a year would be:

Interest = $2,000,000,000 x 0.03 = $60,000,000

Therefore, if the interest rate is 3% per year, the interest on $2 billion would be $60,000,000 for one year.
